g.text: arg2 (rdx) | unsigned long arg (ptr or value)
action | ring 3 → ring 0 via SYSCALL instruction
TLB flush | NO (same process VA space)
common ioctls handled here — never reach your driver
single indirect call through file_operations vtable
Old kernel | .ioctl required Big Kernel Lock (BKL) before calling handler
BKL removed | Linux 2.6.39 — single global mutex that serialized all ioctl
Modern name | .unlocked_ioctl = handler is called WITHOUT any global lock
Implication | YOU are responsible for your own synchronization (mutex)
cmd | _IOW('k',0,ulong) — user WRITES new_size TO kernel
cmd | _IO('k',1) — NO argument: arg is undefined, never deref
arg unused | _IO macro encodes zero size bits
wake write | clearing creates free space → writers can proceed
f_pos | readers must re-open or lseek to see empty buffer
step 2 | build struct mydevice_status on kernel stack
cmd | _IOR('k',2,struct) — kernel WRITES status TO user
meaning | command not recognized by this driver
callers | strace decodes ENOTTY as 'not a typewriter' (historical)
also hit | when _IOC_TYPE != magic OR _IOC_NR > MAXNR
-ERESTARTSYS | signal interrupted wait → errno = EINTR (or restart)
ring 0→3 | CPU privilege drop, return value in rax
